title: oenothera biennis subsp. chicaginensis
intro: Oenothera biennis subsp. chicaginensis, also known as evening primrose, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Onagraceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an essential fatty acid that has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing effects. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including eczema, acne, and menopausal symptoms, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote skin health. Evening primrose oil, which is extracted from the plant's seeds, is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as capsules, creams, and ointments, to support skin health and alleviate various health conditions.
